questions.q: What are the main causes of rural poverty?
questions.answer: Rural poverty is caused by factors such as lack of education, limited access to credit and marketing facilities, low productivity, and inadequate infrastructure. The Green Revolution in India has also widened the rural-urban income gap.
key_points: Limited access to credit and marketing facilities

questions.q: What is the role of rural infrastructure in rural development?
questions.answer: Rural infrastructure such as roads, irrigation, and telecommunications plays a crucial role in rural development by facilitating the delivery of goods and services, improving agricultural productivity, and enhancing the quality of life.
key_points: Facilitating the delivery of goods and services

questions.q: What are the benefits of rural electrification?
questions.answer: Rural electrification has numerous benefits such as improved lighting, increased agricultural productivity, and enhanced quality of life. It also promotes entrepreneurship and economic growth in rural areas.

questions.q: What is the importance of irrigation in rural development?
questions.answer: Irrigation is crucial for rural development as it ensures a stable food supply, increases agricultural productivity, and reduces the risk of crop failure. It also helps in reducing poverty and improving the standard of living.
key_points: Reducing the risk of crop failure
Reducing poverty and improving the standard of living

questions.q: What is the role of cooperatives in rural development?
questions.answer: Cooperatives play a vital role in rural development by providing financial services, improving agricultural productivity, and promoting economic growth. They also help in reducing poverty and improving the standard of living of rural communities.
key_points: Reducing poverty and improving the standard of living

questions.q: What are the main objectives of the Integrated Rural Development Programme (IRDP)?
questions.answer: The main objectives of IRDP are to provide employment opportunities, improve agricultural productivity, and enhance the quality of life of rural communities. It also aims to reduce poverty and promote economic growth in rural areas.
key_points: Reducing poverty and promoting economic growth

questions.q: What is the importance of rural education in rural development?
questions.answer: Rural education is essential for rural development as it improves the quality of life, increases agricultural productivity, and promotes economic growth. It also helps in reducing poverty and improving the standard of living of rural communities.
key_points: Reducing poverty and improving the standard of living

questions.q: What are the main causes of rural-urban migration?
questions.answer: The main causes of rural-urban migration are poverty, lack of employment opportunities, and limited access to education and healthcare in rural areas. It also includes push factors such as poor infrastructure and lack of basic amenities.
key_points: Limited access to education and healthcare
Poor infrastructure and lack of basic amenities

questions.q: What is the role of rural entrepreneurship in rural development?
questions.answer: Rural entrepreneurship plays a vital role in rural development by creating employment opportunities, improving agricultural productivity, and promoting economic growth. It also helps in reducing poverty and improving the standard of living of rural communities.
key_points: Reducing poverty and improving the standard of living

questions.q: What are the main benefits of rural women's empowerment?
questions.answer: The main benefits of rural women's empowerment are improved health and nutrition, increased income and economic independence, and enhanced social status. It also promotes education and literacy among rural women.
